logistics.explanation: Expo and registration at Le Balcon de Villard simplifies pickup in the Vercors massif. Start and finish at Balcon de Villard 5km from Villard-de-Lans town center offers easy access to hotels and festivities. Supported group rides on May 23 at La Place de l'Ours aid pre-race acclimation.

length.explanation: 60 miles through Vercors Regional Nature Park suits mid-distance road riders targeting mountain challenges. Medium course features initial descent from Balcon Villard Cote 2000 before climbs. Long course demands full effort over the plateau's sweeping landscapes.

technicality.explanation: Paved roads carved into Vercors cliffside prioritize endurance over bike handling skills. Steady ascents like Col de la Croix Perrin at 1218m reward power climbing. Final 13km climb to Villard-de-Lans mirrors Tour de France finishes without gravel demands.

elevation.explanation: 5600 feet gain across Vercors gorges and pastures challenges with sustained climbs near Grande Moucherolle at 2285m. Col de la Croix Perrin marks early summit efforts on medium route. Final ascent to Villard-de-Lans tests legs after deep valley traversals.

climate.explanation: May timing in Isere delivers mild weather ideal for mountain road racing near Grenoble. Exceptional Vercors climate supports family activities pre- and post-race. Post-race festivities at Le Balcon de Villard extend the pleasant outdoor experience.

altitude.explanation: High plateau riding in Vercors reaches summits like Col de la Croix Perrin at 1218m. Start at Balcon Villard Cote 2000 elevates early efforts. Proximity to Grande Moucherolle base adds thin-air climbing near 2000m+ terrain.

adventure.explanation: Dramatic Vercors escarpments and limestone cliffs deliver immersive mountain parcours. Routes through deep gorges and green valleys near Villard-de-Lans evoke epic exploration. Mythical landscapes make 5600 feet gain feel like a plateau conquest.

prestige.explanation: GFNY branding brings global qualifiers to Villard-de-Lans. Final climb hosted Tour de France finishes with wins by Laurent Fignon. 2020 stage victory by young German adds historical cachet to the Vercors arrival.

race_quality.explanation: Chip timing and age-group podiums via GFNY system ensure fair regional cyclosportive. Awards at 14:00 on May 24 at Le Balcon de Villard celebrate top finishers. French Cycling Federation oversight maintains standards in Isere.

experience.explanation: Mandatory GFNY jersey designed in NYC highlights pro-level feel in Vercors. Post-race meal from 10:30 and festivities until 17:00 at Balcon de Villard build camaraderie. Welcome Aperitif for VIPs on May 22 enhances immersion.

community.explanation: 1500 participants fill Villard-de-Lans hotels during expo weekend. GFNY Club group rides at La Place de l'Ours foster connections. Family Plus labeled town offers post-race activities in the nature park.

field_depth.explanation: Around 1500 riders create competitive age-group fields in the 1000-5000 range. Top 10% qualifiers motivate mid-pack challengers on Vercors roads. Podium pursuits draw strong regional peloton to Balcon de Villard start.

value.explanation: GFNY jersey, chip timing, and aid stations included for standard entry to 60-mile challenge. Expo access and post-race perks at Le Balcon de Villard add worth. Vercors location near Grenoble balances cost with scenery.

expenses.explanation: Mountain town hotels in Villard-de-Lans during May peak moderately. Travel to Isere from Grenoble Airport keeps costs reasonable for Europeans. VIP upgrades for group rides justify extras without excess.

cultural_impact.explanation: Regional draw in Vercors massif builds local cycling buzz around Villard-de-Lans. Tour de France history elevates Isere's profile modestly. GFNY France events strengthen French cyclosportive scene without national dominance.

course_description.summary: GFNY Villard de Lans winds 60 miles through Vercors Regional Nature Park's cliffside roads, limestone escarpments, and green valleys near Villard-de-Lans. Riders tackle 5600 feet of elevation on paved climbs amid sweeping plateau views. The demanding parcours culminates in a storied final ascent that has hosted Tour de France finishes.

key_segments.name: Col de la Croix Perrin
key_segments.description: First major climb on medium course reaching 1218m summit after descent from Balcon Villard Cote 2000.

key_segments.name: Final Climb to Villard de Lans
key_segments.description: 13km ascent mirroring Tour de France stage finishes through Vercors pastures.

course_description.elevation_profile: Steady climbs through gorges and plateaus with key summits at Col de la Croix Perrin (1218m) and high start at Balcon Villard Cote 2000, totaling 5600 feet over 60 miles.
course_description.aid_stations: Aid stations with amenities featured on course map, supporting long mountain efforts in Vercors.

registration.cost: Standard GFNY fee with VIP upgrade

travel.accommodation: Hotels in Villard-de-Lans town center and resorts like Balcon de Villard

race_day.start_time: Early morning from Balcon de Villard
race_day.categories: Age group podiums, top 10% qualifiers, open 18+
race_day.awards: 14:00 ceremony at Le Balcon de Villard

training_recommendations.minimum_fitness: Base of 2000 miles/year with hill repeats
training_recommendations.ideal_preparation: Build 4-6 weeks of threshold climbs mimicking 8-13km Vercors ascents
training_recommendations.key_workouts: 3x10min at 5% gradient; 2hr rides with 3000ft gain; race-pace 60-mile simulation

history.notable_facts: GFNY expansion into Vercors massif; leverages Tour de France legacy on Villard-de-Lans climb with wins by Laurent Fignon and 2020 German solo victor.

final_verdict.one_liner: Vercors mountain showcase with GFNY polish.
final_verdict.who_should_ride: Climbers seeking European plateau epics under 100 miles.
final_verdict.who_should_skip: Gravel purists or flat sprinters avoiding 5600ft gain.
final_verdict.insider_tip: Acclimate via GFNY Club rides at La Place de l'Ours to nail Col de la Croix Perrin.

suffering_zones.label: Vercors Plateau Grind
suffering_zones.description: Sustained 5600ft accumulation through escarpments and gorges fatigues legs before 13km finale.

suffering_zones.label: Col de la Croix Perrin
suffering_zones.description: 1218m summit early tests power after Cote 2000 descent in thin Vercors air.

suffering_zones.label: Final Tour Climb
suffering_zones.description: 13km to Villard-de-Lans evokes pro suffering on historic paved ramps.
